description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex changes:
  - Add optional `:task_sup` to the `@type input` typespec (`optional(:task_sup) => atom() | pid()`). Production defaults to `MutagenEx.TaskSup`.
  - Replace `Task.async(fn -> ... end)` (line ~175) with `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(task_sup, fn -> ... end)` where `task_sup = Map.get(input, :task_sup, MutagenEx.TaskSup)`. Body inside the closure is unchanged (test_modules add_module loop + try/rescue/catch around `apply(ex_unit, :run, [])`).
  - `Task.yield` call (line ~196): no change. Task.yield speaks monitor; `:DOWN`-vs-`:EXIT` shift is contained.
  - `cancel_on_timeout` (line ~228): thread `task_sup` through as a parameter. Keep `Task.shutdown(task, grace_ms)` for phase 1 (structured-return shape). The `nil` arm calls `brutal_shutdown(task, task_sup)`.
  - `brutal_shutdown` (line ~280): swap `Task.shutdown(task, :brutal_kill)` for `Task.Supervisor.terminate_child(task_sup, task.pid)`. Immediately after `terminate_child`, call `Process.demonitor(task.ref, [:flush])` to prevent stale `:DOWN` accumulation across timeout sites. Preserve the `pre_kill_result = Task.yield(task, 0)` case structure unchanged.
- Per-site task crash path remains: caller observes via Task struct's monitor, arrives at the `{:exit, reason}` case arm in mutation_loop.ex:210-211. No new receive-for-:EXIT anywhere.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - v2 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs) green — outcome tuple shapes unchanged
  - v3, v4 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs, c2_test.exs) green
  - v5 of mutagen.mutation_pipeline (mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs) green
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - Outcome tuple shape for `:timeout` is byte-for-byte identical to pre-`.18` (existing structural assertions in mutation_runner_test.exs continue to hold).
  - r4 (revised) holds: timed-out site classified `:timeout`; next iteration carries `tainted_predecessors: true`; `:code.purge/1` runs on scoped modules before restore.
  - The runner's mailbox stays clean across N consecutive timeout sites (no stale `:DOWN` accumulation). Verifiable via inspection but not a unit-test gate in S2 — S4 scenario 3 + scenario 5 are the structural gates.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (that is S1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (that is S3)
- test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs (that is S4)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md (that is S5)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex internals — `.17`'s `with_restore/4` MUST NOT be touched or duplicated
- everything else in lib/mutagen_ex/

Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (only)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce parallelism on the mutation loop — having Task.Supervisor is the foundation; `.30` builds on it.
- Do not change `MutationLoop.run/1`'s outcome shape — tagged tuples MUST stay identical.
- Do not change `cancel_grace_ms` default (100 ms) or `:shutdown_timeout` (5_000 ms). Tuning is `.30`.
- Do not thread `:task_sup` through `MutationRunner.run/1` — injection is at the MutationLoop boundary only (per refined plan TC-12).
- Do not add a separate receive for `:EXIT` info messages — trust `Task.yield/2` / `Task.shutdown/2`'s monitor handling.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green (mutation_runner_test.exs and integration c1/c2 are the regression gate)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0.jsonl

Commit: 0ef3fe2
Branch: mutagen-wrd.18.2/supervised-loop (stage worktree at /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-supervised-loop)
Base:   epic/mutagen-wrd @ f384c25

Files touched:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex

Tests added: none — S2 is a swap, not a new behavior surface. Existing
v2 (mutation_runner_test.exs), v3 (c1), v4 (c2), v5 (mutation_runner_raise_test.exs)
all still pass and remain the regression gate. S4 (supervision_test.exs) is the
structural gate for the new supervised-kill / descendant-reaping behavior.

Verification stubs flipped: none in S2 (per the ticket — S2 doesn't author new
verification stubs; v6 in mutagen.mutation_pipeline stays `execute: false` until
S4 ships the supervision_test.exs file).

Local verification (in stage worktree):
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: green
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs`: 22 tests, 0 failures
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs`: 2 tests, 0 failures
- `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs`: 7 tests, 0 failures
- `mix test` (full suite): 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded)

Summary: Swapped `MutationLoop`'s per-site task from `Task.async/1` to
`Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(task_sup, fn -> ... end)` (defaulting to
`MutagenEx.TaskSup`, with optional `:task_sup` input for test injection) and
swapped the brutal-cancel phase from `Task.shutdown(task, :brutal_kill)` to
`Task.Supervisor.terminate_child(task_sup, task.pid)` followed by
`Process.demonitor(task.ref, [:flush])`. The `pre_kill_result = Task.yield(task, 0)`
shape is preserved so any racing result is still honored. Phase 1
(`Task.shutdown(task, grace_ms)`) is unchanged for its structured-return shape;
its `{:exit, _}` arms also flush the monitor so the runner's mailbox stays clean
across timeout sites regardless of which phase cleared the task. Outcome-tuple
shape from `MutationLoop.run/1` is byte-for-byte unchanged — confirmed by 357/357
existing tests still passing.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0 (parent) / agent-a1438231e6ddf5565 (this verify subagent)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-a1438231e6ddf5565.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 1
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: v1, v2, v3, v4, v5 (all execute: true). v6 stays execute: false (correctly — it gates on S4).
- Stubs passed: 5 / 5
  - v1 mix test test/mutagen_ex/baseline_test.exs → 8/8
  - v2 mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs → 22/22
  - v3 mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs → 1/1
  - v4 mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c2_test.exs → 1/1
  - v5 mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs → 7/7
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement directly exercised by S2):
  - r4 (Task.Supervisor.async_nolink + two-phase cancel + terminate_child): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs:485 (timeout+taint), :541 (graceful cancel returns :survived), :584 (brutal escalation < 800ms ceiling), :615 (:code.purge settle on :timeout). Would fail if Task.Supervisor.async_nolink raised :noproc against MutagenEx.TaskSup (start ordering broken), if phase-2 weren't invoked (the 800ms brutal-escalation ceiling would blow past the 1_000ms drain), or if the outcome tuple shape shifted (the r1.status/r2.tainted_predecessors pattern-match would crash).
  - r5 (five-outcome classification): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs:485 (:timeout), :603 (:timeout via brutal), and mutation_runner_raise_test.exs (:error/:compile_error paths). Would fail if MutationLoop.run/1's outcome tuple shape changed shape — e.g. an extra/missing meta key on the {:timeout, meta} branch would explode the runner's `case body_result` arm and the assertion `r1.status == :timeout` would FunctionClauseError.
  - r7 (snapshot delta + tainted_predecessors): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs:485 and the L751+ leaked-named-process test. Would fail if a stale :DOWN message survived the demonitor flush and the next site's snapshot saw process registry growth from the bookkeeping.
  - r6, r8, r9, r10, r11 (restore/state-drift/stderr/no-reload/no-disk-mutation): unchanged by S2 — v2/v3/v4 continue to gate. Would fail if a refactor accidentally moved restore or capture_io semantics. None did.
  - r12 (raise/throw/exit inside loaded-mutation window): unchanged by S2 — v5 (raise_test.exs) gates. The `with_restore/4` wrapping is untouched.
  - r13 (Application + MagagenEx.Supervisor + MagagenEx.TaskSup boot): pre-existing from S1 (commit f384c25). Confirmed `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` at mix.exs:34 and {Task.Supervisor, name: MutagenEx.TaskSup} at application.ex:51. MutationLoop's default `task_sup = MagagenEx.TaskSup` consumes it.
  - r14 (descendant reaping via terminate_child propagation): NOT covered by S2 tests — by design. v6 (supervision_test.exs) is execute: false in this ticket; S4 is the structural gate. Ticket's Verification block explicitly says "Verifiable via inspection but not a unit-test gate in S2 — S4 scenario 3 + scenario 5 are the structural gates." The implementation is mechanically present (line 313: Task.Supervisor.terminate_child(task_sup, task.pid)) but its propagation semantics are unverified until S4 lands. This is the planned split, not a gap.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - `with_restore/4` in lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex is untouched (git diff stat: only mutation_loop.ex touched).
  - cancel_grace_ms default remains 100 ms (@default_cancel_grace_ms line 64).
  - Task.Supervisor :shutdown_timeout default unchanged (no override in application.ex).
  - No :task_sup threading through MutationRunner.run/1 — injection point is the MutationLoop input map only (typespec line 116, read line 187).
  - No new `receive` for :EXIT info messages (grep on file shows only doc references).
  - MutationLoop.run/1 outcome shape preserved — 357/357 existing tests pass.
- Project verification: pass.
  - `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`: exit 0.
  - `mix test` (full suite): 357 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none (no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comment on this ticket).

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The grace_ms == 0 escape hatch (cancel_on_timeout/3 fallback clause at L296) routes to brutal_shutdown/2 which now goes through the supervisor — correct path semantically; the ticket explicitly preserves this for tests exercising the supervised-kill-only path.
- Both phase-1 termination arms ({:exit, :killed} and {:exit, _other_reason}) now demonitor with :flush before returning, matching the ticket's "the runner's mailbox stays clean across N consecutive timeout sites" intent. brutal_shutdown/2 also demonitors after terminate_child — three demonitor sites total, covering every path where the task gets cleared by anyone other than the happy-path yield. No unit-test gate today; S4 scenario 5 is its structural home.
- The pre_kill_result = Task.yield(task, 0) capture is preserved in brutal_shutdown/2 (line 312) so a racing result that lands between the grace window expiring and terminate_child firing is still honored — matches the ticket's "Preserve the pre_kill_result = Task.yield(task, 0) case structure unchanged" requirement.

title: S2: mutation_loop.ex async_nolink + terminate_child swap + demonitor fix
